page: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a GPU-accelerated server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features an Intel x86_64 architecture with 24 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB per core. For hardware acceleration, it bundles four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, delivering a combined 64 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1200 GB NVMe SSD.

Performance benchmarks indicate average tier capabilities for general CPU and memory tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 35,340.23 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 41,473.88. However, its GPU-driven capabilities show notable strengths. In LLM inference tests using a medium 7B model, the server achieved top-tier prompt processing speeds of 614.8 tokens/sec and strong text generation speeds of 32.83 tokens/sec. For a smaller 135M model, prompt processing reached a top-tier 8,464.24 tokens/sec. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is measured at 137.58 GB/sec for read operations and 56.27 GB/sec for write operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers high resource density by combining 256 GB of RAM and four discrete GPUs with a shared CPU model, making it a cost-effective option for GPU-intensive tasks that do not require dedicated, high-frequency CPU cores. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and a moderate 2.0 GHz base clock speed, which may limit performance in CPU-bound single-threaded tasks. This configuration is highly suited for multi-GPU workloads, including medium-scale LLM inference, high-density video transcoding, virtual desktop infrastructure, and database operations utilizing its 1200 GB NVMe storage.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 24 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz on an Intel x86_64 architecture. It features 256.0 GB of system memory, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing a total of 64 GB VRAM. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU performance with a Passmark CPU score of 35,340.23, but top-tier results in LLM inference, achieving 614.8 tokens/sec for 7B model prompt processing. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for single-threaded CPU tasks, but the high GPU and memory density makes it a cost-efficient platform for parallel graphics processing, virtual desktop infrastructure, media streaming, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 24 shared Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 64 GB total VRAM for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram features 24 shared Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and four NVIDIA A16 GPUs with 64 GB total VRAM.
tagline: A high-density GPU server combining four Ampere GPUs, 256 GB RAM, and NVMe storage for parallel computing and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing 64 GB of total VRAM
24 shared Intel vCPUs and 256 GB of system memory
1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM prompt processing performance for medium-sized models
Optimized for virtual desktop infrastructure, media transcoding, and machine learning inference
